item.title: Tech stocks tumble for a second day. Here's what's behind the selloff.
item.description: Nvidia, Alphabet and other technology stocks fell as Wall Street shifted from rewarding AI spending to demanding evidence that it will produce outsized returns.

item.title: Senate adopts House-passed Iran resolution in symbolic rebuke of Trump
item.description: The Senate approved a House-passed resolution aimed at reining in President Trump on Iran, marking the first time such a measure has made it through both chambers.

item.title: Judge blocks Trump administration's SNAP limits on sodas, candy
item.description: The ruling deals a setback to the "Make America Healthy Again" campaign, which seeks to curb purchases of foods officials say are unhealthy.

item.title: Trump says Iran agreed to nuclear inspections, but Tehran denies claim
item.description: Iran insists there are no plans for inspections of its bombed nuclear sites, but Trump says Tehran "fully and completely" agreed to let inspectors return.

item.title: Chico library shooting suspect apparently wanted Columbine-type massacre, police say
item.description: Two people were killed and a child was injured in a shooting inside a library in Chico on Monday, officials said.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: How much house can you afford with a $75,000 salary right now?
item.description: Thinking about buying a home? Here's what a $75,000 salary can realistically support in today's market.

item.title: Here's how much interest a $75,000 CD account can earn now
item.description: Interest earnings on a CD account of this size can be substantial. Here's what savers need to know right now.

item.title: Bipartisan housing bill aims to boost homeownership. Could it work?
item.description: The 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, which has rare bipartisan support, would make it harder for major investors to hoard homes.

item.title: 3 money market account features for savers to take advantage of now
item.description: A money market account has multiple advantages for savers in today's unique economic climate.

item.title: What has Britain's likely next prime minister said about Trump?
item.description: Andy Burnham, Britain's likely next leader, hasn't said a lot about President Trump, but his few statements have been critical.

item.title: 5 major changes Trump is making in D.C. during his second term
item.description: President Trump's construction projects include restoring the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, building a 90,000-square-foot White House ballroom and a 250-foot triumphal arch.

item.title: Army looking into using 2 U.S. ranges for testing to mimic Ukraine's conditions
item.description: Army Secretary Dan Driscoll said Tuesday the Army will look into introducing electronic jamming to ranges so industry and Army soldiers can train in areas that simulate battlefield conditions.

item.title: From 2011: Hitmaker Clive Davis
item.description: Multiple Grammy-winning music executive Clive Davis, who helped launch the careers of such artists as Barry Manilow, Bruce Springsteen, Whitney Houston and Alicia Keys, died on June 22, 2026 at age 94. In this Oct. 23, 2011 "Sunday Morning" profile, Anthony Mason talked with Davis about his "accidental" career in music, and his comeback after losing his job as head of Columbia Records in the early '70s. Mason also attended one of Davis' legendary pre-Grammy parties, where Jennifer Hudson spoke about what she'd learned from her mentor.

item.title: Why your online job post is getting ignored (and how to fix it)
item.description: Getting the cold shoulder from candidates? The problem might not be the market. It could be the job post itself.

item.title: New business owner? Here's how to find, screen and hire employees fast
item.description: Hiring your first employees doesn't have to take long. Here's how to move quickly without making costly mistakes.

item.title: Watch: Trump takes questions on Iran during Pennsylvania visit
item.description: President Trump spoke to reporters Tuesday before visiting a Mack Trucks facility in Pennsylvania.

item.title: Future of Iran's nuclear program uncertain amid peace talks
item.description: The future of Iran's nuclear program remains unclear amid recent peace talks, despite President Trump's claims that the country "completely agreed" to nuclear inspections. CBS News' Courtney Kealy has more.

item.title: Why credit card debt relief makes sense this July
item.description: Credit card debt relief may be worth serious consideration this July. Here's what borrowers should know right now.

item.title: Supreme Court rules ex-inmate can't sue prison officials for shaving dreadlocks
item.description: The Supreme Court rejected a former Louisiana inmate's effort to sue state prison officials after they shaved his dreadlocks in violation of his religious beliefs.

item.title: Marco Rubio reacts to Iran's latest contradictions on nuclear weapons as U.S. talks continue
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio spoke to reporters after landing in the United Arab Emirates on Tuesday, where he touted "good progress" with Iran but also said "a lot of work remains to be done."

item.title: Supreme Court bars Rastafarian man from suing prison officials who shaved dreadlocks
item.description: In a 6-3 ruling, the Supreme Court ruled that a devout Rastafari man in Louisiana can't sue the state prison officials who forcibly shaved his dreadlocks. CBS News' chief legal correspondent Jan Crawford reports on the court's most recent and upcoming cases.

item.title: Wyndham Clark addresses backlash at U.S. Open
item.description: Wyndham Clark endured booing all weekend at Shinnecock Hills for his actions at last year's U.S. Open. After clinching the win on Sunday, Clark said he's ready for his redemption tour.

item.title: Wyndham Clark addresses scrutiny after last year's U.S. Open: "Not who I am"
item.description: Wyndham Clark, who won the U.S. Open on Sunday, spoke about the backlash he's received since last year's incident at the U.S. Open, how he gets past the crowd rooting against him and the emotional moment he had celebrating with his dad.

item.title: Critical primary elections underway in New York, South Carolina
item.description: Voters are headed to the polls in New York on Tuesday for key elections. Meanwhile, South Carolina is holding a runoff for Republican gubernatorial candidates. CBS News' Shanelle Kaul and Skyler Henry have more.

item.title: Supreme Court blocks Rastafarian man from suing prison that made him cut his dreadlocks
item.description: The Supreme Court has blocked a Rastafarian man from suing the Louisiana prison officials who forced him to cut off his dreadlocks. CBS News' Jan Crawford has more.

item.title: Clive Davis' extraordinary musical ear led to "one success after another," biographer says
item.description: Record label executive and starmaker Clive Davis died at 94 on Monday. His influence spans genres and decades. Music critic and Davis biographer Anthony DeCurtis reflects on the life and legacy of Clive Davis.

item.title: 5 major Trump projects changing how Washington, D.C., looks
item.description: President Trump is overseeing major construction projects in Washington, D.C. Here are the latest developments as some of the plans navigate contentious legal battles and rising questions about funding. CBS News senior White House and political correspondent Ed O'Keefe reports.

item.title: Nancy Guthrie ransom note, believed to be from abductor, said she died, sources say
item.description: Authorities believe two ransom notes addressed to Nancy Guthrie's family — including a note that said she had died — were likely sent by the person or group of people who abducted her.

item.title: Endangered pregnant whale found dead on bow of cruise ship in Alaska
item.description: A pregnant fin whale was found dead on the bow of a cruise ship in Alaska last week. Fin whales are endangered and particularly threatened by vessel strikes.
